title: mitracarpus hasslerianus
intro: Mitracarpus hasslerianus, also known as "Cachiporã" or "Guaco," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which are responsible for its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, making it useful in preventing and treating diseases such as diabetes, hypertension, and cancer. Herbal preparations of Mitracarpus hasslerianus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or used topically to treat various health conditions.
